name: Multi-tenant SaaS schema design
description: B2B SaaS with multiple tenant companies. Each tenant has users, projects, documents. Need strong isolation so tenant A can never see tenant B's data, but minimize ops cost — cannot create a new DB per tenant. Using Postgres.
task: Design the database schema for a multi-tenant SaaS in Postgres. Requirements: (1) strict tenant isolation — tenant A can NEVER see tenant B's rows, (2) shared DB cluster (not per-tenant DBs), (3) users belong to one tenant, each with a role (admin/member), (4) projects belong to tenants, documents belong to projects, (5) support for tenant-specific settings, (6) efficient queries on tenant-scoped data. Provide: the DDL for tables, any indexes, RLS policies if using row-level security, and a sample query showing how the app ensures tenant isolation.
expected_outcomes: Explicit tenant_id column on every tenant-scoped table
Proper foreign key relationships with ON DELETE behavior
Index strategy that includes tenant_id first for query performance
Row-level security policies (or equivalent app-layer enforcement)
Sample SELECT showing tenant_id filter or SET role
Handles role-based access within a tenant
